title: Aswan → Luxor
summary: A short, refined sailing experience for travellers with limited time — the temples and ancient quarries of the Nile between Aswan and Luxor, unhurried, in three nights.
description: Timeless charm meets the gentle rhythm of the river. This express voyage carries you north from Aswan to Luxor, visiting the distinctive double temple of Kom Ombo, the great sandstone quarries of Gebel el-Silsila, the superbly preserved Temple of Horus at Edfu, and the ancient city of El Kab — with traditional sailing and long, relaxed hours on deck.
routeOverview: Three nights aboard your dahabiya, sailing north with the current from Aswan to Luxor. Highlights include Kom Ombo, the sandstone quarries of Gebel el-Silsila, the Temple of Horus at Edfu, and the ancient city of El Kab, with a passage through the Esna Lock before arriving in Luxor — the ancient city of Thebes.

timeline.title: Embarkation in Aswan — Kom Ombo & Gebel el-Silsila
timeline.description: Your journey begins in Aswan, where you board the vessel around midday before sailing north along the Nile. Enjoy lunch and your first views of traditional Nile villages and desert landscapes, then visit the remarkable Temple of Kom Ombo before continuing to the dramatic sandstone cliffs of Gebel el-Silsila.

schedule.text: Begin sailing toward Kom Ombo (approx. 4 hours downstream with the Nile current). Enjoy lunch and your first views of traditional Nile villages and desert landscapes.

Visit the remarkable Temple of Kom Ombo, uniquely dedicated to two gods: Sobek, the crocodile god, and Horus the Elder. The temple's symmetrical design and beautifully carved reliefs make it one of the most distinctive temples in Egypt.
Continue sailing north to Gebel el-Silsila (approximately 3 hours).
Moored at Gebel el-Silsila, surrounded by the dramatic sandstone cliffs that once supplied stone for many of Egypt's great temples.

timeline.title: Gebel el-Silsila — Edfu & El Kab
timeline.description: This morning explores one of the Nile Valley's lesser-visited but historically important quarry sites, before sailing on to the best-preserved temple in Egypt at Edfu and continuing toward the ancient city of El Kab.

schedule.text: Visit Gebel el-Silsila, the principal sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt. Explore the rock-cut shrines, stelae, and quarries where blocks were extracted for temples across the country.

Visit the magnificent Temple of Horus at Edfu, the best-preserved temple in Egypt. Built during the Ptolemaic period, its towering pylons, monumental statues of Horus, and detailed reliefs illustrate ancient rituals and mythological scenes.
Resume sailing toward El Kab (about 4 hours).
El Kab, near the ancient city of Nekheb, once an important religious centre dedicated to the vulture goddess Nekhbet.

timeline.title: Esna & Sailing to Luxor
timeline.description: An early start allows for smooth navigation through the Nile's only lock on this section of the river, followed by a full day's sail through the fertile Nile Valley to Luxor.

schedule.text: Early morning sailing toward Esna (approximately 2 hours).
Passage through the Esna Lock, an interesting experience as the vessel is raised to match the water level upstream.
Continue sailing north toward Luxor (approximately 6 hours), enjoying the scenery of the Nile Valley with its fertile fields, palm groves, and riverside villages.

timeline.title: Disembarkation in Luxor
timeline.description: After breakfast, disembark in Luxor — the ancient city of Thebes and one of the world's greatest archaeological regions.

schedule.text: After breakfast on board, disembark in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile journey.

faq.q: Is this itinerary too short to see the highlights?
faq.a: Not at all — three nights covers a rich sequence of Nile temples and sites: Kom Ombo, Gebel el-Silsila, Edfu, and El Kab, ending in Luxor. It is ideal for travellers with limited time who still want the full dahabiya experience.

Your cabin, all meals featuring local and international cuisine, the daily sunset afternoon tea on deck, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, guides, and transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your charter.

faq.q: Can we sail from Luxor to Aswan instead?
faq.a: Yes. The same highlights can be arranged in the reverse direction to suit your arrival city — just let us know and we will tailor it.
